### Hourly Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — every 4 h. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

### Combine Probe Results

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: All Healthy?

### All Healthy?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($json.statusCode || 200) < 400 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Record Uptime Metric · ②→ Already Alerted? (State Check)

### Record Uptime Metric

Appends a new row to 'Page Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Stamp Processing Time · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Already Alerted? (State Check)

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Page On-Call via Slack

### Page On-Call via Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#seo`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Stamp Processing Time

Stamps `processedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Guard Non-Empty url

### Guard Non-Empty url

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Sort Pages Newest First

### Sort Pages Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

Appends a new row to 'Page Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Discord · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Discord

### Escalate Failure to Discord

Posts to a Discord channel via an incoming webhook — no bot token needed, the webhook URL on the credential is the only secret.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Discord webhook URL

# Report on site health with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive

**Pattern:** SEO Audit · **Level:** Advanced · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Report on site health with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual backlinks is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Hourly Schedule → Probe Endpoint A → Combine Probe Results → All Healthy? → Record Uptime Metric → Stamp Processing Time → Tag Pipeline Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to seo audit without manual effort
- Anyone automating backlinks in marketing
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Discord webhook URL; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

'Already Alerted?' uses cross-execution dedupe specifically so a service that's down for an hour pages on-call once, not on every scheduled probe — alert fatigue is a real failure mode, and this node is the guard against it.

## Practice exercises

You've got the basics covered — push further:

1. Add a third endpoint to probe and watch the Merge node's input count update.
2. Point a probe at a URL that returns 500 and confirm exactly one page fires, not one per scheduled check.
3. Change the alert dedupe key so alerts re-fire daily instead of once per incident.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your backlinks process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
